summary: Added a Path Map Route Handoff card that surfaces the lane owner, thread, gate/note/urgency context, and stages the lane's top browser-local dispatch draft from the route board.

queries: 2026 operations dashboard handoff panel command center UX best practices
incident command dashboard handoff notes action owner UX patterns
dashboard design action cards owner next action notes best practices 2026
pattern_notes: Command surfaces should expose ownership, active pressure, and the next action close to the operator's current context.
Operational dashboards need handoff continuity, especially where blockers, notes, and decisions can otherwise scatter across views.
Action controls should be local, explicit, and reversible when the system is not allowed to contact agents or external services by itself.

ui_contract: Render a Path Map Route Handoff card next to blocker and note context.
Show the owner bot or lane manager, linked thread, gate count, local note count, and dispatch urgency.
Use the existing best lane dispatch suggestion when available, falling back to laneDispatchSuggestion.
Stage only a browser-local dispatch draft through the existing outbox and history keys.
Re-render Path Map after staging so the handoff card shows the OK/staged state.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation the Path Map had no .path-handoff-panel, no data-path-handoff-stage control, and the dispatch outbox remained empty.

green_route.evidence: Clicking Q from Route Handoff wrote one browser-local gate_review draft for digital_products_templates_plugins, added one history event, flipped the panel to staged/OK, and produced no console errors, failed requests, or overflow.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered the Route Handoff panel, staged one local draft, and reported no horizontal overflow or bad rectangles.
